title: alnus glutinosa subf. lobulata
intro: Alnus glutinosa subf. lobulata, also known as the common alder, is a subspecies of the deciduous tree Alnus glutinosa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alnus glutinosa subf. lobulata include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in wound healing. Alnus glutinosa subf. lobulata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and tinctures, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional before consumption or application.
